justification: All pass criteria met: 12-month growth projection calculated (8.9x, 445K orders/day); database identified as primary bottleneck (70% CPU, full table scans); 85% disk utilization flagged as critical with 2-3 month timeline; index fix identified as highest ROI first step (Week 1 emergency); phased plan present (indexes → read replicas → partitioning → CQRS); sharding explicitly deferred ('Sharding — premature at this scale, massive engineering cost'); trade-offs include replication lag and cache invalidation complexity (via CQRS/read model staleness discussion).

observations.text: Disk emergency clearly flagged as critical severity with concrete math (70GB free, 30-40GB/month growth, 2-3 months to full)
Index fix is explicitly the first action item (Week 1 emergency) with estimated 15-25% CPU relief
Phased plan is well-structured with realistic engineer allocation for a 4-person team
Sharding explicitly rejected as premature, consistent with team size constraints
Replication lag explicitly called out as medium-severity risk with practical mitigation
Goes beyond requirements with connection pooling analysis, PgBouncer timing, and concrete migration file list

Caching for customer order history not explicitly recommended as a medium-term step — instead proposes CQRS read models in Phase 3, which is a reasonable alternative but diverges from the expected cache-based approach
Correctly identifies API servers are NOT the bottleneck — scales them only after database work is done
